top_signals.title: How Ukraine Put Russia on the Backfoot
top_signals.summary:
Ukraine’s expanding use of drones at all ranges has changed the war’s momentum. This episode examines how Kyiv is reaching deeper into Russian territory, disrupting operations and logistics as well as energy infrastructure. How is Russia adapting (or not)? Can these strikes produce lasting advantages on the battlefield? Will this force Russia to escalate? Mike Kofman and Dara Massicot join Ryan to discuss.This episode is brought to you by Booz Allen. Learn more at warontherocks.com/boozallen.Image: ArmyInform

The post How Ukraine Put Russia on the Backfoot appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Street flip: Intermodal rail charges ahead in latest data
top_signals.summary:
Containers and trailers hauled by rail powered ahead while carload shipments were narrowly off in the latest U.S. data.

The post Street flip: Intermodal rail charges ahead in latest data appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Financial Services Sector Seen as Focus for Activist Campaigns
top_signals.summary: Financial services companies are attracting activist investors who see them as generating cash that could be paid out to investors, said Paul Weiss’ Carmen Lu.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: EM on customs duty on distance imported goods sales (C(2026)2760)
top_signals.summary: The government's summary of a Commission Delegated Regulation as regards elements related to the temporary customs duty on distance sales of imported goods.
